memoryArtifacts.headline: History of Maunsell or Mansel (Page 7)
memoryArtifacts.description:
https://archive.org/details/historyofmaunsel00maun/page/n33/mode/2up?view=theater
Citation
History of Maunsell or Mansel, and of Crayford, Gabbett, Knoyle, Persse, Toler, Waller, Castletown
Waller, Prior Park; Warren, White, Winthrop, and Mansell of Guernsey.
Comp. by Robert George Maunsell.
Published 1903 by Guy and Company, Limited in Cork .
Written in English.
Page 7

zh.lifeSummary: Gervaise de Pagnell是Fulk Paganell的兒子，於大約1098年出生在英國武斯特斯爾杜德利。他和Baroness Cecily FitzAnsculph至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於大約1153年在他的家鄉享年57歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Pagnell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English female personal name Pernel, Parnell (Old French Peronel(le)) from Latin Petronilla, a feminine pet form of the Latin personal name Petronius. Petronilla was the name of a supposed early Christian saint and martyr. This surname was also brought to Ireland from Cheshire in the 17th century, by a family who had been supporters of Cromwell's Parliamentarians during the English Civil War. Charles Stewart Parnell, who founded the Irish Parliamentary Party in 1882, was a descendant of this family. See also Pennell .

variants.name: Penn
meanings: English: habitational name from either of two places called Penn (in Buckinghamshire and Staffordshire), both of which are named with Brittonic penn ‘head’, often used in hill-names.

English: topographic name or metonymic occupational name from Middle English pen(n) ‘enclosure, animal pen, fold’ (Old English penn), for someone who lived by or worked at a fold. Examples of this surname are formally difficult to distinguish from those in 1 above.
English: from a pet form of the female personal name Pernel, see Parnell .

variants.name: Pernell
meanings: English (Middlesex and Warwickshire): variant of   Parnell  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Pennell
meanings: metronym from the Middle English female personal name Pennel (an assimilated form of Parnell ).

habitational name from Penn Hall in Pensax (Worcestershire), or from either of two places called Penhill in Devon and Yorkshire, or possibly from Pendle in Lancashire. All of the placenames probably derive from British penn ‘head’ + Old English hyll ‘hill’.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: Gervaise de Pagnell was born about 1098, in Dudley, Worcestershire, England as the son of Fulk Paganell. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Baroness Cecily FitzAnsculph. He died about 1153, in his hometown, at the age of 57.

ja.lifeSummary: Gervaise de Pagnellは約1098年にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアダドリーでFulk Paganellの息子として生まれた。彼はBaroness Cecily FitzAnsculphとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は約1153年に故郷で57歳で死去した。

ko.Dudley Castle, Worcestershire, , England: 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 드들리 드들리 카스틀
ko.lifeSummary: Gervaise de Pagnell(은)는 대략 1098년 영국 워스스터실르 드들리에서 Fulk Paganell의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Baroness Cecily FitzAnsculph(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1153년 그의 고향에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.
